record_identity.title: Canonical code taxonomy consolidation for CAM operations and provenance codes

summary: Consolidate canonical code taxonomy across the Caelestis Repository to align single-letter global codes with the OPERATIONS taxonomy; introduce structured Provenance canonical codes (PINT, PTRUST) and harmonize existing single-letter codes into a stable multi-character namespace.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Inconsistent single-letter global codes create operational risk for automated tooling, governance mapping, and cross-repository integration; consolidation will reduce misclassification, enable machine validation, and satisfy OPERATIONS taxonomy requirements.
proposal_rationale: CAM and Caelestis repositories need a stable canonical code namespace so single-letter legacy codes do not conflict with OPERATIONS taxonomy terms or future provenance and trust-state codes.

source_records.relevance_note: Observed presence of single-letter global codes across the Caelestis repository; causes mapping inconsistencies with OPERATIONS taxonomy.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Inventory legacy single-letter global codes, map them into a stable multi-character namespace, introduce structured provenance codes such as PINT and PTRUST, and harmonize repository references.
automation_components: validators or scripts that check canonical code references
interface_components: public code labels and filters after taxonomy stabilization

suggested_insertion_points: Create a repository-wide inventory of canonical code fragments.
Draft namespace mapping rules before making instrument patches.

external_relevance.external_summary: Primarily internal to CAM/Caelestis governance infrastructure, with downstream relevance for public registry consistency and standards-facing terminology.

next_action: Conduct repository maintenance to consolidate canonical codes: 1) inventory all single-letter global codes across Caelestis repositories; 2) map each to a stable multi-character namespace that aligns with the OPERATIONS taxonomy; 3) formally introduce PINT (Provenance Intensity) and PTRUST (Provenance Reliance Tier) canonical codes into the global registry; 4) update tooling and validators to accept the new namespace; 5) publish migration guidance and a compatibility window. Provisional provenance axes and values to be included in migration notes: Continuity Depth: transient (provisional) — transient ↔ persistent identity; Propagation Risk: isolated (provisional) — isolated ↔ reusable/exportable; Harm Domain: casual (provisional) — casual ↔ financial/medical/security; Attribution Sensitivity: low (provisional) — low ↔ economically/legally meaningful; Identity Formation Intensity: exploratory (provisional) — exploratory ↔ continuity-bearing; Automation Reliance: advisory (provisional) — advisory ↔ machine-executed; Public Legitimacy Impact: private (provisional) — private ↔ reputational/civil. Canonical codes (provisional): PINT=medium; PTRUST=tier-2. Notes: values marked 'provisional' pending formal calibration; this is a Repo Maintenance action to harmonize codes and satisfy OPERATIONS taxonomy requirements.
migration_notes: V2 fields added by additive migration; legacy flat fields preserved for compatibility.
Normalized to the clean VIGIL record design: source_records is the only canonical source-evidence block in individual records; source_data is not used.
